title: phlox carolina var. triflora
intro: Phlox carolina var. triflora, also known as three-flowered phlox, is a type of flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. It belongs to the polemoniaceae family and is often used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering potential health benefits like reduced inflammation and improved wound healing. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a range of applications.
